channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Wizards finally hold a team under 100 points, the last NBA team to do so this season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It took 57 games for the Washington Wizards to hold a team under 100 points this season. Of course, Marcus Smart wasn't around for most of those. Smart and the Wizards held Brooklyn to 12 points in the fourth quarter of their 107-99 victory over the Nets on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Matthew Stafford wants $50 million to play in 2025 as Rams trade looms: report
item.description: Matthew Stafford, who could be traded from the Los Angeles Rams this offseason, reportedly wants to be paid $50 million or more to play in the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pat Kraft discusses Beaver Stadium, Thon and NIL in first address since December
item.description: UNIVERSITY PARK (Nittany Nation) -- Penn State athletic director Pat Kraft met with reporters Monday for the first time since the Big Ten Championship in December. He spoke for about 40 minutes diving deep on topics like NIL, College Football Playoff expansion, and even Thon.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Clay, PHL 17

item.title: Ross Chastain hopes Austin gives him another reason to destroy a watermelon
item.description: The eighth-generation watermelon farmer from Florida won his first Cup Series race at Circuit of the Americas in 2022, and after every win, he pays homage to his roots by slamming a melon like a medicine ball on the track.
item.dc:creator: Billy Gates, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Al Trautwig, famed NYC sports broadcaster, dead at 68
item.description: Al Trautwig, a famed New York City sportscaster who covered the top teams in the region, has died at 68. His friends and former colleagues mourned the loss on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travis Hunter's possible double duty gives the NFL's annual scouting combine a different look
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Travis Hunter will arrive at the NFL's annual scouting combine listed as a cornerback. By the end of this week, he could be so much more.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas is No. 1 in women's AP Top 25 for the first time in 21 years; NC State climbs into top 10
item.description: Texas moved up to No. 1 in The Associated Press women's basketball poll for the first time in 21 years after Notre Dame lost in overtime to N.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panthers will not re-sign longtime linebacker Shaq Thompson
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — The Carolina Panthers are moving on from longtime linebacker Shaq Thompson. The Panthers informed Thompson, who was set to become an unrestricted free agent, that they will not be re-signing him. Thompson is expected to pursue other options in free agency.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Logan Paul 'never doing' at least 1 high-risk move in WWE again
item.description: Logan Paul has become one of the top heels in WWE since he made his first appearance in 2021. His wrestling moves landed him credibility with the audience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brian Campbell cashes in on a big break and wins Mexico Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: VALLARTA, Mexico (AP) — Brian Campbell cashed in on a huge break Sunday when his tee shot in a playoff bounced out of the trees and back into play, leading to a birdie on the second extra hole to win the Mexico Open over Aldrich Potgieter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alex Rodriguez makes half-court shot, wins Bucknell student $10,000
item.description: Former Major League Baseball star Alex Rodriguez showed his skill on the hardwood Sunday, making a half-court shot to win a Bucknell student $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: University of Arizona apologizes for offensive chants aimed at BYU basketball team
item.description: The University of Arizona Athletics Department is apologizing after a derogatory chant aimed at BYU players ensued at a men's basketball game Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Booker, Durant and Beal help the Suns beat the Bulls to snap a 4-game losing streak
item.description: Devin Booker had 29 points and eight assists, Kevin Durant scored 27 points and Bradley Beal had 25 in his return to the starting lineup to help the Phoenix Suns beat the Chicago Bulls 121-117 on Saturday to end a four-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe, an 8-year NFL veteran who played his entire career with the Minnesota Vikings, took to the podium to voice his opposition to the plaque design.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Ticket demand rapidly rising for Luka Doncic's revenge game against the Dallas Mavericks
item.description: The moment that fans of the NBA have been waiting for is just days away, and the prices to get in the arena are meeting the hype. New Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic, who was acquired in a shocking trade on Feb. 1, will have his chance soon to take on his old squad.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Utah State blasts San Jose State, 105-57
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State basketball team got back on track following Sunday's heartbreaking loss to New Mexico with a resounding beatdown over San Jose State Wednesday night, 105-57.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: 1st-year White Sox manager Will Venable goes 1-on-1 with GN Sports from spring training
item.description: PHOENIX — As first-year White Sox manager Will Venable begins to introduce himself to Chicago baseball fans, you can probably already state this about him with certainty: He'd beat any other MLB manager on the basketball court.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
